data.title: Unilever weighs RMT-structured deal with McCormick to give shareholders stake in new entity -- report
data.description:
Unilever’s (UL) discussions to spin off then sell its food business to McCormick could include a provision for Unilever’s (UL) shareholders to obtain a majority stake in the new entity, according to sources cited by Reuters.

The British conglomerate received an offer for its food business last week from McCormick (MKC) for an unspecified amount. 

The proposed deal would spin off the food business before selling it to McCormick, arranged as a Reverse Morris Trust ((RMT)), a common reorganization strategy when the buyer is smaller than the seller. The RMT also allows a company to divest a subsidiary to a third party without incurring capital gains taxes. Under Section 355 (e) of the IRS Code, RMT deals are structured to give shareholders of the parent company at least 50% ownership in the combined entity.

Unilever’s (UL) food business is reportedly valued between $32B and $35.8B, including debt, while McCormick’s (MKC) enterprise value is pegged at $18B, including debt.

Reuters’ sources claim negotiations are progressing “quickly.”

Shares of Unilever (UL) and McCormick (MKC) are both higher on Friday by 0.20%  and 3%, respectively.

data.title: SANUWAVE outlines 16–25% revenue growth target for 2026 as reseller expansion accelerates
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SANUWAVE Health, Inc. (SNWV) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Morgan Frank reported "Q4 was an all-time record for SANUWAVE with revenues of $13.4 million, up 30% versus the same quarter last year and adjusted EBITDA of $4.8 million, up from $3.7 million in the prior year and comprising 36% of revenues." He noted the sale of 624 UltraMIST systems for the year, with Q4 setting a company record at 255 systems sold. Frank highlighted the sunset of dermaPACE and Profile product lines in Q4 and acknowledged that "charges in Q4 increased cost of goods sold and OpEx."
  • Frank addressed industry-wide CMS reimbursement changes in skin substitutes and allografts, clarifying "none of those changes to reimbursement pertain to UltraMIST or to the 97610 code...But no company is an island and anything that affects the practitioners in this space affects everyone." He explained that the changes reduced customer and patient counts, impacting growth rate, but observed "green shoots" as new mobile wound providers emerged and existing customers expanded.
  • Frank announced a strategic push: "We have engaged with a number of resellers to kind of add to our feet on the street count and go get after this current opportunity," emphasizing "land grabs are speed moves" and noting 32% of Q4 revenues came through outside resellers and distributors, up from 26% the prior quarter.
  • CFO Peter Sorensen stated, "We delivered a strong fourth quarter with revenue reaching a new all-time quarterly high and growing 30% year-over-year. This performance reflects continued execution of our commercial strategy and increase in demand for UltraMIST, particularly driven by higher consumables utilization and continued system placements."
  • Sorensen reported a gross margin of 77% for the full year, with Q4 gross margin at 74.7% due to a $486,000 write-off from sunsetting the PACE line. He disclosed a restatement related to "previously unrecognized sales tax liabilities" and incremental expenses totaling $1.6 million in both 2024 and 2025.

Outlook

  • Frank provided guidance: "we're guiding to $9.6 million to $10.3 million in Q1 revenues. This is up 3% to 10% from the prior year...We expect this to get better going forward...providing a preliminary 2025 estimate range of 16% to 25% revenue growth for the year 2025 versus 2024."
  • Management described early signs of recovery from industry disruption, referencing increased inbound interest and hiring momentum.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue reached $13.4 million, a 30% increase year-over-year. Full year revenue was $44.1 million, up 35% from 2024.
  • Gross margin for Q4 was 74.7%, impacted by inventory write-offs; without this, gross margin would have been 78.3%.
  • Net income for Q4 was $7.7 million, up from a net loss of $13.3 million in Q4 2024, driven primarily by a $5.9 million non-cash gain in fair value of derivative liabilities.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $4.8 million, and total current assets were $24.6 million at year-end with $12 million in cash.

Q&A

  • Carl Byrnes, Northland Capital Markets: Asked about the selling cycle for new inbound interest and timing of adjusted EBITDA guidance. Frank said the sales cycle "stretched" but is "starting to get better" and that incremental revenue might "drop by something on the order of 50% to the EBITDA line."
  • Kyle Bauser, ROTH Capital Partners: Asked about quarterly growth cadence, commercial headcount, and reseller margins. Frank confirmed Q1 is expected to be below full-year growth rates and stated internal sales force is "at 14 or 15," with reseller partnerships growing in both number and size. He noted operating margin for reseller sales is "a bit higher in terms of operating margin than our W2 sales."
  • Ian Cassel, IFCM: Inquired about Q4 system placements and manufacturing updates. Frank explained the 255 systems sold were "an unusually large number of kind of midsized orders" with a net 5% increase in active systems due to discontinued customers. He mentioned manufacturer price reductions and ongoing delays in qualifying a new applicator line.
  • Albert Hanser asked about key industry events, with Frank highlighting SANUWAVE's participation in SAWC in April.
  • An unknown attendee asked about discontinued systems and UltraMIST consumable usage rates. Frank attributed discontinuations to customer shutdowns, usually due to financial distress, and said consumable usage rates vary by customer type.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly positive, focusing on operational details, growth cadence, and margin structure. Questions sought clarity on headcount, margins, and customer churn but did not press on negative surprises.
  • Management's sentiment was confident in prepared remarks, with Frank stating "there is a land grab going on to see who gets to serve" the market. In Q&A, management remained measured, avoiding specifics on EBITDA guidance and production ramp timing, occasionally expressing caution due to industry uncertainty.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment shifted from cautious optimism to guarded confidence, with both sides acknowledging ongoing industry disruption but noting early signs of stabilization.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted from Q3's $13–$14 million revenue range to a Q1 2026 range of $9.6–$10.3 million, indicating an expected slowdown due to CMS-driven disruption but a rebound later in 2026 with 16–25% full-year growth targeted.
  • Strategic focus moved from managing uncertainty to aggressive reseller expansion and adaptation to new customer segments, labeled "baby elephants."
  • Analysts in both quarters highlighted channel strategy and headcount, but the current quarter featured more questions on system discontinuations and customer churn.
  • Management's confidence in long-term positioning increased, though near-term caution was more pronounced due to the evolving reimbursement environment.

Risks and Concerns

  • CEO Frank detailed industry-wide CMS reimbursement cuts and aggressive audits, leading to customer shutdowns and churn: "individual wound care providers get hit with 9-figure clawbacks on skin substitutes."
  • The company removed 168 systems from its active count in Q4 due to discontinued customers, signaling ongoing market turbulence.
  • Sorensen cited ongoing remediation of sales tax liabilities, with potential for some additional expense in Q1 as state and local issues are resolved.
  • Management is focused on maintaining channel inventory discipline and mitigating the risk of inventory build-up with resellers.

Final Takeaway

Management reported a record quarter in revenue and system placements while acknowledging significant headwinds from CMS reimbursement changes and customer churn. The company is targeting 16–25% revenue growth for 2026 and is aggressively expanding its reseller network to capture new market opportunities. Despite near-term industry disruption, SANUWAVE sees early signs of recovery and is positioning itself to benefit from shifts toward evidence-based, cost-effective wound care solutions.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Rocket Pharma wins FDA nod for gene therapy (update)
data.description:
Rocket Pharmaceuticals (RCKT) added ~9% in the premarket on Friday after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved its gene therapy Kresladi for a rare immune disease called leukocyte adhesion deficiency-I (LAD-I).

However, the stock fell 17% in the morning hours after the company conducted a conference call on the approval, which allows it to secure a rare pediatric disease priority review voucher from the regulator.

The FDA issued accelerated approval for the drug to treat children with LAD-I due to biallelic variants in the ITGB2 gene. The patients without a specific donor for a stem cell transplant will be eligible to receive Kresladi.

The approval comes after the Cranbury, New Jersey-based biotech resubmitted a biologics license application for Kresladi, as the FDA declined to approve the drug in June 2024, requesting additional data to complete the review.

In accepting the BLA in October, the FDA had issued March 28, 2026, as the target action date for the review.

The regulator said that Kresladi, also known as marnetegragene autotemce, will be the first gene therapy indicated for LAD-I. The immune deficiency is caused by mutations in the ITGB2 gene, which encodes a protein important for white blood cells to fight infection.

In a statement, Rocket Pharma (RCKT) said it would review strategic options to monetize the PRV in a bid to increase its financial flexibility and maximize shareholder value. "We look forward to making KRESLADI available to eligible patients in the United States," CEO Gaurav Shah added.

Update: 11.40 am ET: Adds latest stock move and comments from Rocket Pharma

data.title: EU officials say Iran war raising risk of stagflation
data.description:
The European Union is facing an increased risk of stagflation due to ongoing conflict in the Middle East, according to EU officials.

“It is clear that, in any scenario, we are at real risk of a stagflationary shock,” Valdis Dombrovskis, the EU's commissioner for economy and productivity, told reporters Friday, according to Bloomberg. “The outlook is clouded by profound uncertainty."

According to European Commission estimates, the Middle East war could reduce EU output by 0.4% this year if it is relatively short. A longer war could lower output by 0.6% for both 2026 and 2027. Inflation could be pushed as much as 1.5% higher in 2026, depending on the war's duration, Bloomberg noted. 

Dombrovskis's concerns echo those of EU Governing Council member Boris Vujcic, who said earlier this week that the European Central Bank must be "very agile and vigilant" as the risk of stagflation grows due to the Iran war.  Vujcic added that ECB officials will know soon whether rates will need to be raised 

“We do not see stagflation, but the risk is moving into the direction of stagflation,” Vujcic told Bloomberg in an interview earlier this week. “How far we’ll get in that direction is very difficult to predict.”

Vujcic is slated to become vice president of the ECB in June.

data.title: Humana is the worst performing large-cap healthcare stock YTD
data.description:
U.S. large-cap healthcare stocks have delivered modest year-to-date gains in 2026, holding steady into March amid its defensive appeal.

Below is a list of the 10 worst-performing large-cap healthcare stocks year-to-date, ranked by their YTD performance as of late March 2026. The list includes companies from various healthcare subsectors with large market capitalizations.

The list is led by Humana Inc. (HUM), with a YTD performance of -31.62%. Boston Scientific Corporation (BSX) and IQVIA Holdings Inc. (IQV) follow closely behind, with declines of -26.41% and -24.85%, respectively.

Other notable companies experiencing significant YTD declines include Insulet Corporation (PODD) and Waters Corporation (WAT), which have dropped -23.27% and -20.87%, respectively. Despite the price drops, all stocks on this list currently maintain a Quant rating of “Hold.”

Here is the list:

  1. Humana Inc. (HUM), YTD perf: -31.62%, Quant rating: 2.77

  2. Boston Scientific Corporation (BSX), YTD perf: -26.41%, Quant rating: 2.88

  3. IQVIA Holdings Inc. (IQV), YTD perf: -24.85%, Quant rating: 2.71

  4. Insulet Corporation (PODD), YTD perf: -23.27%, Quant rating: 3.06

  5. Waters Corporation (WAT), YTD perf: -20.87%, Quant rating: 3.05

  6. Veeva Systems Inc. (VEEV), YTD perf: -20.14%, Quant rating: 3.21

  7. Becton, Dickinson and Company (BDX), YTD perf: -18.91%, Quant rating: 2.61

  8. Centene Corporation (CNC), YTD perf: -18.44%, Quant rating: 3.32

  9. Solventum Corporation (SOLV), YTD perf: -18.34%, Quant rating: 3.17

  10. Danaher Corporation (DHR), YTD perf: -17.85%, Quant rating: 3.01

Health Care ETFs: (XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), and (FXH)

data.title: Oil markets are seeing ‘the biggest gap in energy supplies the world’s ever seen’ – analyst
data.description:
The world is facing “the biggest gap in energy supplies the world’s ever seen” as the war with Iran enters its 29th day, according to Francisco Blanch, head of commodities and derivatives research at Bank of America.

With Brent crude (CO1:COM) trading at $110 per barrel, Blanch warned in an interview with CNBC that the global oil buffer is rapidly depleting and the market has only about ten to fourteen days before hitting a critical shortage.

Blanch laid out what he called “basic oil math,” explaining that roughly 700M barrels of oil—300M barrels in transit from previously sanctioned Russian and Iranian supplies, plus 400M barrels from released reserves—are serving as the world’s current cushion.

“The market obviously is pretty anxious,” Blanch said, noting that the system is “chewing through the spare buffer” with approximately 20M barrels flowing daily through the Strait of Hormuz.

Even if the conflict ends, Blanch cautioned that oil volumes may not return to full capacity due to Iran’s demonstrated ability to threaten energy infrastructure and the strait itself.

He pointed to the Bab el-Mandeb Strait as a cautionary example, where Houthi interference reduced flows from 9M barrels per day to roughly 4M—a drop of more than 50%.

“That was done with relatively basic technology,” Blanch noted.

The physical market is already showing signs of strain, with record oil and jet fuel prices emerging in the East. Some countries without adequate fuel supplies have already begun rationing, implementing four-day workweeks, and encouraging remote work.

“We don’t really know how much flexibility there is in the system,” Blanch said, adding that “very, very high prices” will be required to manage the supply shortfall.

Looking ahead, Blanch indicated that prices could go “a lot higher” if military action escalates with a potential ground invasion. However, even in the event of a ceasefire, he expressed doubt that prices would fall significantly.

“I think we’ll be lower if we’ve de-escalated, but I don’t think we’ll be getting back to $60 a barrel or even $70 anytime soon,” he concluded.

Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).

data.title: Paranovus announces 1-for-12 reverse share split; shares down
data.description:
• Paranovus Entertainment Technology (PAVS) will implement a 1-for-12 reverse share split of its Class A ordinary shares.
  • The adjusted shares will begin trading on Nasdaq Capital Market on March 31, 2026.
  • Outstanding shares will decrease from about 11.34M to ~944,778 after the split.
  • PAVS shares down 16%.

data.title: Apple gives iPhone designers stock bonuses to keep them from leaving: report
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) has granted some iPhone designers bonuses in stock worth up to hundreds of thousands of dollars in an attempt to keep them from departing the tech giant, Bloomberg reported.

The bonuses, which are out of the normal cycle for the iPhone maker, were made to members of the iPhone Product Design team, the news outlet added, citing people with knowledge of the matter. Executives at the firm have become increasingly concerned about Apple's employees being hired by rivals, including OpenAI (OPENAI), which has poached several Apple employees in recent months.

The bonuses were granted to members of the Product Design team as stock units, and they have a vesting schedule over four years, the news outlet added. Some awards are presently worth between $200,000 and $400,000.

The iPhone 17 lineup has resonated well with consumers, accounting for $85.26B in revenue in Apple's most recent quarter. CEO Tim Cook said consumers showed “unprecedented demand” for the iPhone in the period ending Dec. 27.

Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

OpenAI also reportedly made a “conscious decision” to sit out competing for the chance to run Siri with its own artificial intelligence models, amid increased competition between the two firms. The deal went to Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), though Apple and OpenAI do have a working relationship, with ChatGPT currently being used to help provide user queries that Siri is unable to answer.

Former Apple designer Sir Jony Ive joined OpenAI in May 2025 to work on a new AI device. Not much is known about the new device, though some have suggested one of the potential offerings is a smart speaker that may be priced between $200 and $300. 

OpenAI's chief global affairs officer Chris Lehane said in January that OpenAI is “on track” to show off the device later this year.
